title: Request Construction, Method Semantics, Safety, and Idempotency · Browser and Node Client Systems · Spiral Review
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for request construction, method semantics, safety, and idempotency, producing method-aware request builder. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch method-aware request builder after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Distinguish safe and idempotent method semantics from what one particular server happens to do. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use GET and HEAD with conditional, range, cache, and body expectations that preserve their defined semantics. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Request Components · changed case · Review Request Components
steps.instruction:
review case — offline design-system client must repeat request T-2 after an uncertain timeout: GET status, POST job creation, PUT replacement, and PATCH transition are candidates. Classify safety, idempotency, body replay, and If-Match evidence before choosing. Competency probe: Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Which action demonstrates Request Components without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Request Components · changed case · Review Request Components.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Request Components · changed case · Review Request Components, keep Request Components connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
